key_insight: Off-the-shelf multimodal LLMs can zero-shot identify stress-critical 3D regions from physics-guided prompts, making them a plausible semantic proxy for mesh refinement.
engineering_relevance: This could reduce reliance on iterative PDE-based adaptivity or large supervised surrogates, helping target FEM refinement more efficiently under a fixed compute budget.
method: Uses multimodal LLMs as zero-shot visual semantic assistants for mesh refinement
Adds orthoViews to select 2D viewpoints that better expose important 3D geometric features
Compares against a tuned geometric heuristic under matched refinement budgets
evidence_signal: The abstract reports an empirical study across diverse CAD geometries, loading cases, and SOTA MLLMs, with results showing higher precision than blind heuristics at identifying stress-relevant features.
watch_item: Performance still depends on robust view selection and 2D-to-3D grounding, so the approach may be less reliable for occluded or ambiguous geometries.
paper_title: GReFEM: Multimodal LLMs as Zero-Shot Semantic Assistants for Physics-Guided 3D Mesh Refinement
